name: N.O. Port Commission Employees
description_short: Credit union serving Port of New Orleans employees since 1952, offering checking, savings, ATM/debit cards, and loan products with member-focused rates and personalized service.
description_long:
N.O. Port Commission Employees Credit Union has been serving the employees of the Port of New Orleans for over 70 years, established in 1952. As a member-owned financial cooperative, the institution is structured to prioritize the financial well-being of its membership rather than external shareholders. The credit union operates from its headquarters at the Port of New Orleans location and maintains a physical presence accessible to members in the New Orleans area.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of banking services including checking and savings accounts, ATM/debit card access via Visa, cashier's checks, money orders, ACH processing for automatic payments, and online banking capabilities. All members opening a checking account receive a Visa ATM/debit card. Additional services include ACH pre-authorization for recurring payments to savings and checking accounts, enabling convenient automated financial management. The organization emphasizes accessibility through multiple contact channels including phone, fax, email, and physical locations.

The institution's primary distinction is its employee-membership model focused on Port of New Orleans workers, which theoretically allows for more favorable interest rates on loans, higher rates on savings products, and lower fees compared to traditional banks. The credit union positions itself on competitive advantage through personalized customer service and member-centric pricing. As a credit union rather than a commercial bank, it operates under different regulatory frameworks and profit-sharing models that can benefit members.

Based on available website information, this is a straightforward community credit union with standard retail banking services. The website lacks detailed information about loan products, interest rate specifics, fee structures, minimum balances, or membership eligibility beyond port employee status. No information about credit-building products, specialized lending programs, or competitive rate data is publicly available. The organization appears to be a modest-sized institution focused on serving its defined membership base rather than pursuing broad market expansion.
services: Checking accounts with Visa ATM/debit card issuance
ACH processing and automatic payment pre-authorization
Account access through physical branch location
pros: Established 70-year history serving Port of New Orleans employees since 1952, indicating stability and institutional knowledge
Member-owned credit union structure designed to return profits to members rather than external shareholders
Complimentary Visa ATM/debit card issued with all new checking accounts
Offers online banking for remote account management and monitoring
ACH processing and automatic payment pre-authorization for convenient recurring transactions
Lower fees and higher savings rates positioned relative to traditional banks
Multiple contact methods including phone, email, fax, and physical location access
Personalized customer service model emphasizing member relationships over transaction volume
cons: Membership restricted to Port of New Orleans employees, limiting accessibility to general public
Limited information on website regarding specific loan products, interest rates, terms, or APR disclosures
Minimal fee schedule transparency—only cashier check fee ($7.00) disclosed on website
No information about minimum account balances, savings rate details, or CD products
Website provides no details about membership eligibility requirements or account opening process
best_for: Current and retired employees of the Port of New Orleans seeking convenient banking services
Port employees needing personalized financial service from a member-owned institution
Members prioritizing local credit union banking over national bank alternatives
Port workers seeking to consolidate payroll and banking services with institution familiar with port operations
diagnosis: This credit union is best for Port of New Orleans employees seeking basic retail banking services from a member-owned institution with a 70-year track record. The primary limitation is membership restriction to port employees and the lack of detailed public information about loan products, rates, and fees—interested parties must contact the institution directly for comprehensive service and pricing details.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
